```{r model11-interpretation}
  cat(sprintf("    combined knowledge score increases by %.2f points (out of 10)\n", beta_cocreation_t8_combined))
  cat(sprintf("  - This represents a %.1f%% increase per co-creation level\n", beta_cocreation_t8_combined/10*100))
  cat(sprintf("  - Effect per item: %.3f points\n", beta_cocreation_t8_combined/10))
} else {
  cat("  [NO] NO significant effect of co-creation on T8 combined knowledge\n")
}

# Compare with T7 combined (Model 10)
cat("\nComparison with T7 Combined Knowledge (Model 10):\n")
cat(sprintf("  T7: beta = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", beta_cocreation_t7_combined, p_value_t7_combined))
cat(sprintf("  T8: beta = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", beta_cocreation_t8_combined, p_value_t8_combined))

if (p_value_t7_combined < 0.05 && p_value_t8_combined < 0.05) {
  retention_pct_combined <- (beta_cocreation_t8_combined / beta_cocreation_t7_combined) * 100
  cat(sprintf("\n  Effect Retention: T8 is %.1f%% of T7 effect\n", retention_pct_combined))
  
  if (retention_pct_combined > 90) {
    cat("  -> EXCELLENT retention of overall knowledge benefits\n")
  } else if (retention_pct_combined > 70) {
    cat("  -> GOOD retention of overall knowledge benefits\n")
  } else if (retention_pct_combined > 50) {
    cat("  -> MODERATE retention of overall knowledge benefits\n")
  } else {
    cat("  -> WEAK retention of overall knowledge benefits\n")
  }
} else if (p_value_t7_combined < 0.05 && p_value_t8_combined >= 0.05) {
  cat("\n  -> Overall knowledge benefits DID NOT persist\n")
} else if (p_value_t7_combined >= 0.05 && p_value_t8_combined < 0.05) {
  cat("\n  -> Delayed emergence of overall knowledge effects\n")
}

# Compare with component models at T8
cat("\nComparison with Component Models (T8):\n")
cat(sprintf("  Basic only (Model 7, 2 items): beta = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", 
            fixef(model7)["Cocreation_Level"], 
            summary(model7)$coefficients["Cocreation_Level", "Pr(>|t|)"]))
cat(sprintf("  LLM only (Model 9, 8 items): beta = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", 
            fixef(model9)["Cocreation_Level"], 
            summary(model9)$coefficients["Cocreation_Level", "Pr(>|t|)"]))
cat(sprintf("  Combined (Model 11, 10 items): beta = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", 
            beta_cocreation_t8_combined, p_value_t8_combined))

# Random effects
var_group_t8_combined <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(model11))$vcov[1]
var_resid_t8_combined <- attr(VarCorr(model11), "sc")^2
icc_val_t8_combined <- var_group_t8_combined / (var_group_t8_combined + var_resid_t8_combined)

cat("\nRandom Effects:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Group variance: %.3f\n", var_group_t8_combined))
cat(sprintf("  Residual variance: %.3f\n", var_resid_t8_combined))
cat(sprintf("  ICC: %.3f (%.1f%% between groups)\n", icc_val_t8_combined, icc_val_t8_combined*100))

# Model fit
r2_vals_t8_combined <- r2(model11)
cat(sprintf("\nModel Fit:\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Marginal R²: %.3f\n", r2_vals_t8_combined$R2_marginal))
cat(sprintf("  Conditional R²: %.3f\n", r2_vals_t8_combined$R2_conditional))
cat(sprintf("  AIC: %.1f, BIC: %.1f\n", AIC(model11), BIC(model11)))

cat("\n================================\n")
```

### Model 11 Visualization

```{r model11-plot, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
plot_model(model11, type = "pred", terms = "Cocreation_Level") +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted T8 Combined Knowledge by Co-creation Level",
    subtitle = "Model 11: Combined score (2 basic + 8 LLM = 10 total items, delayed)",
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Predicted Combined Score (0-10)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

### Combined Knowledge Comparison: T7 vs T8

```{r model10-model11-comparison, fig.width=14, fig.height=6}
# Create comparison plots for T7 vs T8 combined scores
library(gridExtra)

# T7 combined plot
p_t7_combined <- plot_model(model10, type = "pred", terms = "Cocreation_Level") +
  labs(
    title = "T7: Immediate (Combined 10 items)",
    subtitle = paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model10)[2], 3), 
                     ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model10))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Predicted Score (0-10)"
  ) +
  ylim(0, 10) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# T8 combined plot
p_t8_combined <- plot_model(model11, type = "pred", terms = "Cocreation_Level") +
  labs(
    title = "T8: Delayed (Combined 10 items)",
    subtitle = paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model11)[2], 3), 
                     ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model11))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Predicted Score (0-10)"
  ) +
  ylim(0, 10) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# Arrange side by side
grid.arrange(p_t7_combined, p_t8_combined, ncol = 2,
             top = "Combined Microcontroller Knowledge: Immediate vs Delayed Effects")
```

```{r combined-t7-t8-raw-comparison, fig.width=14, fig.height=6}
# Raw data comparison
df_t7_t8_combined <- df_long %>% 
  filter(Timepoint %in% c("T7", "T8"), !is.na(combined_score))

ggplot(df_t7_t8_combined, aes(x = Cocreation_Level, y = combined_score)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Timepoint_factor), width = 0.1, height = 0.15, alpha = 0.6, size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color = Timepoint_factor, fill = Timepoint_factor), 
              method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("T7" = "#10b981", "T8" = "#8b5cf6"),
                    labels = c("T7 (Immediate)", "T8 (Delayed)")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("T7" = "#10b981", "T8" = "#8b5cf6"),
                   labels = c("T7 (Immediate)", "T8 (Delayed)")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.5, 10.5), breaks = seq(0, 10, 2)) +
  labs(
    title = "Combined Microcontroller Knowledge: Immediate (T7) vs Delayed (T8)",
    subtitle = "Total score: 2 basic + 8 LLM = 10 items",
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Combined Knowledge Score (0-10)",
    color = "Timepoint",
    fill = "Timepoint"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

## Model 12: Knowledge Change (T7 → T8) - Does Co-creation Protect Against Decay?

Does co-creation level predict how much knowledge students retain or lose from T7 to T8? This model examines whether higher co-creation protects against knowledge decay.

```{r model12-knowledge-change}
cat("\n=== MODEL 12: KNOWLEDGE CHANGE ANALYSIS ===\n\n")

# Analyze all three types of knowledge change
cat("Data for knowledge change analysis:\n")
cat("  Students with complete T7→T8 data:", nrow(df_change), "\n")
cat("  Students with LLM data:", sum(!is.na(df_change$llm_change)), "\n")
cat("  Students with combined data:", sum(!is.na(df_change$combined_change)), "\n\n")

# Model 12a: Basic microcontroller knowledge change
cat("=== MODEL 12a: Basic Knowledge Change ===\n")
model12a <- lmer(
  basic_change ~ Cocreation_Level + (1 | Group_factor),
  data = df_change,
  REML = TRUE
)

summary(model12a)

tidy(model12a, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Model 12a: Co-creation → Basic Knowledge Change (T7→T8)")

# Model 12b: LLM microcontroller knowledge change
cat("\n=== MODEL 12b: LLM Knowledge Change ===\n")
model12b <- lmer(
  llm_change ~ Cocreation_Level + (1 | Group_factor),
  data = df_change %>% filter(!is.na(llm_change)),
  REML = TRUE
)

summary(model12b)

tidy(model12b, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Model 12b: Co-creation → LLM Knowledge Change (T7→T8)")

# Model 12c: Combined knowledge change
cat("\n=== MODEL 12c: Combined Knowledge Change ===\n")
model12c <- lmer(
  combined_change ~ Cocreation_Level + (1 | Group_factor),
  data = df_change %>% filter(!is.na(combined_change)),
  REML = TRUE
)

summary(model12c)

tidy(model12c, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Model 12c: Co-creation → Combined Knowledge Change (T7→T8)")
```

### Model 12 Interpretation

```{r model12-interpretation}
cat("\n=== MODEL 12 COMPREHENSIVE INTERPRETATION ===\n")
cat("Research Question: Does co-creation level predict knowledge change (retention/decay)?\n\n")

# Extract key statistics for all three sub-models
beta_basic_change <- fixef(model12a)["Cocreation_Level"]
p_basic_change <- summary(model12a)$coefficients["Cocreation_Level", "Pr(>|t|)"]
ci_basic_change <- confint(model12a, parm = "Cocreation_Level", level = 0.95)

beta_llm_change <- fixef(model12b)["Cocreation_Level"]
p_llm_change <- summary(model12b)$coefficients["Cocreation_Level", "Pr(>|t|)"]
ci_llm_change <- confint(model12b, parm = "Cocreation_Level", level = 0.95)

beta_combined_change <- fixef(model12c)["Cocreation_Level"]
p_combined_change <- summary(model12c)$coefficients["Cocreation_Level", "Pr(>|t|)"]
ci_combined_change <- confint(model12c, parm = "Cocreation_Level", level = 0.95)

# Overall summary
mean_basic_change <- mean(df_change$basic_change, na.rm = TRUE)
mean_llm_change <- mean(df_change$llm_change, na.rm = TRUE)
mean_combined_change <- mean(df_change$combined_change, na.rm = TRUE)

cat("OVERALL KNOWLEDGE CHANGE (T7 → T8):\n")
cat(sprintf("  Basic questions: mean change = %.3f (%.1f%% of max)\n", 
            mean_basic_change, mean_basic_change/2*100))
cat(sprintf("  LLM questions: mean change = %.3f (%.1f%% of max)\n", 
            mean_llm_change, mean_llm_change/8*100))
cat(sprintf("  Combined: mean change = %.3f (%.1f%% of max)\n", 
            mean_combined_change, mean_combined_change/10*100))

if (mean_basic_change < 0 | mean_llm_change < 0 | mean_combined_change < 0) {
  cat("\n  -> On average, students experienced KNOWLEDGE DECAY from T7 to T8\n")
} else if (mean_basic_change > 0 | mean_llm_change > 0 | mean_combined_change > 0) {
  cat("\n  -> On average, students experienced KNOWLEDGE GAIN from T7 to T8\n")
} else {
  cat("\n  -> On average, students MAINTAINED knowledge from T7 to T8\n")
}

cat("\n" %>% strrep(2))
cat("A. BASIC MICROCONTROLLER KNOWLEDGE CHANGE:\n")
cat("=" %>% strrep(60), "\n")
cat(sprintf("Beta = %.3f, 95%% CI [%.3f, %.3f], p = %.4f\n", 
            beta_basic_change, ci_basic_change[1], ci_basic_change[2], p_basic_change))

if (p_basic_change < 0.05) {
  cat("[YES] SIGNIFICANT effect of co-creation on basic knowledge change\n\n")
  if (beta_basic_change > 0) {
    cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
    cat("  -> Higher co-creation is associated with LESS DECAY (or more gain)\n")
    cat("  -> For each 1-unit increase in co-creation level,\n")
    cat(sprintf("     students retain/gain %.3f more points from T7 to T8\n", beta_basic_change))
    cat("  -> Co-creation appears to PROTECT against knowledge loss\n")
  } else {
    cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
    cat("  -> Higher co-creation is associated with MORE DECAY (or less gain)\n")
    cat("  -> This suggests potential issues with co-creation implementation\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("[NO] NO significant effect of co-creation on basic knowledge change\n\n")
  cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
  cat("  -> Co-creation level does not predict knowledge retention/decay\n")
  cat("  -> Decay rates are similar across co-creation levels\n")
}

cat("\n\nB. LLM MICROCONTROLLER KNOWLEDGE CHANGE:\n")
cat("=" %>% strrep(60), "\n")
cat(sprintf("Beta = %.3f, 95%% CI [%.3f, %.3f], p = %.4f\n", 
            beta_llm_change, ci_llm_change[1], ci_llm_change[2], p_llm_change))

if (p_llm_change < 0.05) {
  cat("[YES] SIGNIFICANT effect of co-creation on LLM knowledge change\n\n")
  if (beta_llm_change > 0) {
    cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
    cat("  -> Higher co-creation is associated with BETTER RETENTION of LLM content\n")
    cat(sprintf("  -> For each 1-unit increase in co-creation level,\n"))
    cat(sprintf("     students retain/gain %.3f more points from T7 to T8\n", beta_llm_change))
    cat("  -> Co-creation particularly protects LLM-specific knowledge\n")
  } else {
    cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
    cat("  -> Higher co-creation is associated with MORE DECAY of LLM content\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("[NO] NO significant effect of co-creation on LLM knowledge change\n\n")
  cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
  cat("  -> Co-creation level does not predict LLM knowledge retention/decay\n")
}

cat("\n\nC. COMBINED KNOWLEDGE CHANGE:\n")
cat("=" %>% strrep(60), "\n")
cat(sprintf("Beta = %.3f, 95%% CI [%.3f, %.3f], p = %.4f\n", 
            beta_combined_change, ci_combined_change[1], ci_combined_change[2], p_combined_change))

if (p_combined_change < 0.05) {
  cat("[YES] SIGNIFICANT effect of co-creation on overall knowledge change\n\n")
  if (beta_combined_change > 0) {
    cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
    cat("  -> Higher co-creation is associated with BETTER OVERALL RETENTION\n")
    cat(sprintf("  -> For each 1-unit increase in co-creation level,\n"))
    cat(sprintf("     students retain/gain %.3f more points overall (out of 10)\n", beta_combined_change))
    cat("  -> Co-creation provides protective effect across all content\n")
  } else {
    cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
    cat("  -> Higher co-creation is associated with MORE OVERALL DECAY\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("[NO] NO significant effect of co-creation on overall knowledge change\n\n")
  cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
  cat("  -> Co-creation level does not predict overall knowledge retention/decay\n")
}

cat("\n\nD. COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS:\n")
cat("=" %>% strrep(60), "\n")

# Compare effect sizes across knowledge types
cat("Effect sizes (beta coefficients) for knowledge change:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Basic (2 items): %.3f, p = %.4f\n", beta_basic_change, p_basic_change))
cat(sprintf("  LLM (8 items): %.3f, p = %.4f\n", beta_llm_change, p_llm_change))
cat(sprintf("  Combined (10 items): %.3f, p = %.4f\n", beta_combined_change, p_combined_change))

# Per-item comparison
cat("\nPer-item effect sizes:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Basic: %.3f per item\n", beta_basic_change/2))
cat(sprintf("  LLM: %.3f per item\n", beta_llm_change/8))
cat(sprintf("  Combined: %.3f per item\n", beta_combined_change/10))

# Determine if protective effect is uniform
if (p_basic_change < 0.05 && p_llm_change < 0.05) {
  if (beta_basic_change > 0 && beta_llm_change > 0) {
    cat("\n-> Co-creation provides UNIFORM PROTECTIVE effect across both question types\n")
  } else if (beta_basic_change > 0 && beta_llm_change <= 0) {
    cat("\n-> Co-creation protects basic knowledge but NOT LLM-specific knowledge\n")
  } else if (beta_basic_change <= 0 && beta_llm_change > 0) {
    cat("\n-> Co-creation protects LLM knowledge but NOT basic knowledge\n")
  }
} else if (p_basic_change < 0.05 && p_llm_change >= 0.05) {
  cat("\n-> Protective effect is SPECIFIC to basic microcontroller knowledge\n")
} else if (p_basic_change >= 0.05 && p_llm_change < 0.05) {
  cat("\n-> Protective effect is SPECIFIC to LLM microcontroller knowledge\n")
}

# Model fit statistics
cat("\n\nModel Fit Statistics:\n")
cat(sprintf("Model 12a (Basic): R² = %.3f, AIC = %.1f\n", 
            r2(model12a)$R2_marginal, AIC(model12a)))
cat(sprintf("Model 12b (LLM): R² = %.3f, AIC = %.1f\n", 
            r2(model12b)$R2_marginal, AIC(model12b)))
cat(sprintf("Model 12c (Combined): R² = %.3f, AIC = %.1f\n", 
            r2(model12c)$R2_marginal, AIC(model12c)))

cat("\n================================\n")
```

### Model 12 Visualizations

```{r model12-plots, fig.width=16, fig.height=12}
# Create comprehensive visualization of knowledge change
library(gridExtra)

# Plot 1: Predicted knowledge change by co-creation (Basic)
p1 <- plot_model(model12a, type = "pred", terms = "Cocreation_Level") +
  labs(
    title = "Basic Knowledge Change (T7→T8)",
    subtitle = paste0("beta = ", round(beta_basic_change, 3), 
                     ", p = ", format.pval(p_basic_change, digits = 3)),
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Knowledge Change (points)"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = min(df_change$Cocreation_Level), y = 0.1, 
           label = "No change", hjust = 0, color = "red") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# Plot 2: Predicted knowledge change by co-creation (LLM)
p2 <- plot_model(model12b, type = "pred", terms = "Cocreation_Level") +
  labs(
    title = "LLM Knowledge Change (T7→T8)",
    subtitle = paste0("beta = ", round(beta_llm_change, 3), 
                     ", p = ", format.pval(p_llm_change, digits = 3)),
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Knowledge Change (points)"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = min(df_change$Cocreation_Level, na.rm = TRUE), y = 0.3, 
           label = "No change", hjust = 0, color = "red") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# Plot 3: Predicted knowledge change by co-creation (Combined)
p3 <- plot_model(model12c, type = "pred", terms = "Cocreation_Level") +
  labs(
    title = "Combined Knowledge Change (T7→T8)",
    subtitle = paste0("beta = ", round(beta_combined_change, 3), 
                     ", p = ", format.pval(p_combined_change, digits = 3)),
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Knowledge Change (points)"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = min(df_change$Cocreation_Level, na.rm = TRUE), y = 0.3, 
           label = "No change", hjust = 0, color = "red") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# Plot 4: Raw data - Basic change
p4 <- ggplot(df_change, aes(x = Cocreation_Level, y = basic_change)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, height = 0.05, alpha = 0.6, size = 3, color = "#3b82f6") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#1e40af", fill = "#93c5fd", alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Basic: Raw Data",
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Change (T8 - T7)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# Plot 5: Raw data - LLM change
p5 <- ggplot(df_change %>% filter(!is.na(llm_change)), 
             aes(x = Cocreation_Level, y = llm_change)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, height = 0.1, alpha = 0.6, size = 3, color = "#10b981") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#047857", fill = "#6ee7b7", alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "LLM: Raw Data",
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Change (T8 - T7)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# Plot 6: Raw data - Combined change
p6 <- ggplot(df_change %>% filter(!is.na(combined_change)), 
             aes(x = Cocreation_Level, y = combined_change)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, height = 0.15, alpha = 0.6, size = 3, color = "#8b5cf6") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#6d28d9", fill = "#c4b5fd", alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Combined: Raw Data",
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Change (T8 - T7)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# Arrange in 3x2 grid
grid.arrange(p1, p4, p2, p5, p3, p6, ncol = 2,
             top = "Knowledge Change Analysis: Does Co-creation Protect Against Decay?")
```

```{r model12-distribution, fig.width=14, fig.height=6}
# Distribution of knowledge change by co-creation level
df_change_long <- df_change %>%
  select(Cocreation_Level, basic_change, llm_change, combined_change) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(basic_change, llm_change, combined_change),
               names_to = "Knowledge_Type",
               values_to = "Change") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Change)) %>%
  mutate(
    Knowledge_Type = factor(Knowledge_Type,
                           levels = c("basic_change", "llm_change", "combined_change"),
                           labels = c("Basic (2 items)", "LLM (8 items)", "Combined (10 items)"))
  )

ggplot(df_change_long, aes(x = factor(Cocreation_Level), y = Change, fill = Knowledge_Type)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 1) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Knowledge_Type, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#3b82f6", "#10b981", "#8b5cf6")) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Knowledge Change (T7→T8) by Co-creation Level",
    subtitle = "Red line = no change. Above = gain, Below = loss",
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Knowledge Change (T8 - T7)",
    fill = "Knowledge Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

## Model 13: Learning Gains (T1 → T7) - Does Co-creation Predict Learning?

Does co-creation level predict how much students learn from baseline (T1) to immediate post-test (T7)? This model examines whether higher co-creation is associated with greater learning gains.

```{r model13-learning-gains}
cat("\n=== MODEL 13: LEARNING GAINS ANALYSIS (T1 → T7) ===\n\n")

cat("Data for learning gains analysis:\n")
cat("  Students with complete T1→T7 data:", nrow(df_gains), "\n")
cat("  Mean baseline (T1):", round(mean(df_gains$score_t1), 2), "\n")
cat("  Mean post-test (T7):", round(mean(df_gains$score_t7), 2), "\n")
cat("  Mean learning gain:", round(mean(df_gains$learning_gain), 2), "\n\n")

# Model 13: Learning gains (T1 → T7)
model13 <- lmer(
  learning_gain ~ Cocreation_Level + (1 | Group_factor),
  data = df_gains,
  REML = TRUE
)

summary(model13)

tidy(model13, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Model 13: Co-creation → Learning Gains (T1→T7, Basic 2 items)")

# Model performance
cat("\nModel Performance:\n")
performance::icc(model13)
performance::r2(model13)
```

### Model 13 Interpretation

```{r model13-interpretation}
cat("\n=== MODEL 13 INTERPRETATION ===\n")
cat("Research Question: Does co-creation predict learning gains from baseline to post-test?\n\n")

# Extract key statistics
beta_learning_gain <- fixef(model13)["Cocreation_Level"]
se_learning_gain <- sqrt(diag(vcov(model13)))["Cocreation_Level"]
t_value_learning_gain <- summary(model13)$coefficients["Cocreation_Level", "t value"]
p_value_learning_gain <- summary(model13)$coefficients["Cocreation_Level", "Pr(>|t|)"]
ci_learning_gain <- confint(model13, parm = "Cocreation_Level", level = 0.95)

# Overall learning statistics
mean_gain <- mean(df_gains$learning_gain, na.rm = TRUE)
pct_with_gain <- sum(df_gains$learning_gain > 0) / nrow(df_gains) * 100

cat("OVERALL LEARNING PATTERN (T1 → T7):\n")
cat(sprintf("  Mean learning gain: %.3f points (out of 2 possible)\n", mean_gain))
cat(sprintf("  This represents %.1f%% of the maximum possible improvement\n", mean_gain/2*100))
cat(sprintf("  Percentage of students with positive gains: %.1f%%\n", pct_with_gain))

if (mean_gain > 0.5) {
  cat("\n  -> Substantial learning occurred from baseline to post-test\n")
} else if (mean_gain > 0) {
  cat("\n  -> Modest learning occurred from baseline to post-test\n")
} else {
  cat("\n  -> Minimal or no learning detected from baseline to post-test\n")
}

cat("\n\nCO-CREATION EFFECT ON LEARNING GAINS:\n")
cat("=" %>% strrep(60), "\n")
cat(sprintf("Beta = %.3f, SE = %.3f\n", beta_learning_gain, se_learning_gain))
cat(sprintf("95%% CI: [%.3f, %.3f]\n", ci_learning_gain[1], ci_learning_gain[2]))
cat(sprintf("t-value = %.3f, p-value = %.4f\n", t_value_learning_gain, p_value_learning_gain))

if (p_value_learning_gain < 0.05) {
  cat("\n[YES] SIGNIFICANT effect of co-creation on learning gains\n\n")
  if (beta_learning_gain > 0) {
    cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
    cat("  -> Higher co-creation is associated with GREATER LEARNING GAINS\n")
    cat(sprintf("  -> For each 1-unit increase in co-creation level,\n"))
    cat(sprintf("     students gain %.3f more points from T1 to T7\n", beta_learning_gain))
    cat(sprintf("  -> This represents %.1f%% additional learning per co-creation level\n", 
                beta_learning_gain/2*100))
    cat("\n  PRACTICAL SIGNIFICANCE:\n")
    
    # Calculate difference between high and low co-creation
    gain_diff_0_to_3 <- beta_learning_gain * 3
    cat(sprintf("  -> Students at co-creation level 3 vs. level 0 would be expected to\n"))
    cat(sprintf("     learn %.3f more points (%.1f%% more of the scale)\n", 
                gain_diff_0_to_3, gain_diff_0_to_3/2*100))
  } else {
    cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
    cat("  -> Higher co-creation is associated with SMALLER LEARNING GAINS\n")
    cat("  -> This unexpected finding warrants investigation\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("\n[NO] NO significant effect of co-creation on learning gains\n\n")
  cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
  cat("  -> Co-creation level does not predict how much students learn\n")
  cat("  -> Learning gains are similar across co-creation levels\n")
  cat("  -> Other factors (not measured here) drive learning differences\n")
}

# Compare with Model 1 (T7 absolute scores)
beta_t7_absolute <- fixef(model1)["Cocreation_Level"]
p_t7_absolute <- summary(model1)$coefficients["Cocreation_Level", "Pr(>|t|)"]

cat("\n\nCOMPARISON WITH ABSOLUTE T7 SCORES (Model 1):\n")
cat("=" %>% strrep(60), "\n")
cat(sprintf("Absolute T7 scores (Model 1): beta = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", 
            beta_t7_absolute, p_t7_absolute))
cat(sprintf("Learning gains T1→T7 (Model 13): beta = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", 
            beta_learning_gain, p_value_learning_gain))

if (p_t7_absolute < 0.05 && p_value_learning_gain < 0.05) {
  cat("\nBoth models show significant co-creation effects:\n")
  if (abs(beta_t7_absolute - beta_learning_gain) < 0.1) {
    cat("  -> Effect sizes are SIMILAR\n")
    cat("  -> This suggests co-creation primarily affects learning gains\n")
    cat("  -> (not just baseline differences)\n")
  } else if (beta_learning_gain > beta_t7_absolute) {
    cat("  -> STRONGER effect on learning gains than absolute scores\n")
    cat("  -> Co-creation particularly impacts the learning process\n")
  } else {
    cat("  -> WEAKER effect on learning gains than absolute scores\n")
    cat("  -> Some of the T7 effect may reflect baseline differences\n")
  }
} else if (p_t7_absolute < 0.05 && p_value_learning_gain >= 0.05) {
  cat("\nT7 effects exist but not explained by learning gains:\n")
  cat("  -> The T7 co-creation effect may be due to baseline differences\n")
  cat("  -> See Model 2 for selection effects analysis\n")
} else if (p_t7_absolute >= 0.05 && p_value_learning_gain < 0.05) {
  cat("\nLearning gains show effects not visible in absolute T7:\n")
  cat("  -> Co-creation affects learning trajectory\n")
  cat("  -> Gain scores reveal effects obscured in absolute scores\n")
}

# Random effects
var_group_gain <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(model13))$vcov[1]
var_resid_gain <- attr(VarCorr(model13), "sc")^2
icc_val_gain <- var_group_gain / (var_group_gain + var_resid_gain)

cat("\n\nRandom Effects:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Group variance: %.3f\n", var_group_gain))
cat(sprintf("  Residual variance: %.3f\n", var_resid_gain))
cat(sprintf("  ICC: %.3f (%.1f%% between groups)\n", icc_val_gain, icc_val_gain*100))

if (icc_val_gain > 0.1) {
  cat("  -> Substantial clustering: learning gains vary by group\n")
} else {
  cat("  -> Minimal clustering: learning gains primarily individual\n")
}

# Model fit
r2_vals_gain <- r2(model13)
cat(sprintf("\nModel Fit:\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Marginal R²: %.3f (variance explained by co-creation)\n", r2_vals_gain$R2_marginal))
cat(sprintf("  Conditional R²: %.3f (variance explained by full model)\n", r2_vals_gain$R2_conditional))
cat(sprintf("  AIC: %.1f, BIC: %.1f\n", AIC(model13), BIC(model13)))

cat("\n================================\n")
```

### Model 13: Correlation Analysis

Given the non-significant mixed model result but substantial group-level variance, let's examine the simple correlation and explore the clustering pattern.

```{r model13-correlation-analysis}
cat("\n=== SIMPLE CORRELATION ANALYSIS ===\n")
cat("Examining relationship without mixed model structure\n\n")

# Simple Pearson correlation (student-level)
cor_result <- cor.test(df_gains$Cocreation_Level, df_gains$learning_gain, 
                       method = "pearson")

cat("STUDENT-LEVEL CORRELATION:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Pearson r = %.3f\n", cor_result$estimate))
cat(sprintf("  95%% CI: [%.3f, %.3f]\n", cor_result$conf.int[1], cor_result$conf.int[2]))
cat(sprintf("  t(%d) = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", cor_result$parameter, cor_result$statistic, cor_result$p.value))

if (cor_result$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("\n  [SIGNIFICANT] Simple correlation exists at student level\n")
} else {
  cat("\n  [NOT SIGNIFICANT] No simple linear relationship at student level\n")
}

# Spearman correlation (non-parametric, in case of non-linearity)
cor_spearman <- cor.test(df_gains$Cocreation_Level, df_gains$learning_gain, 
                         method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)

cat("\nSPEARMAN RANK CORRELATION (non-parametric):\n")
cat(sprintf("  Spearman rho = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", 
            cor_spearman$estimate, cor_spearman$p.value))

# Group-level correlation
df_gains_group <- df_gains %>%
  group_by(Group_factor) %>%
  summarise(
    Cocreation_Level = first(Cocreation_Level),
    mean_gain = mean(learning_gain, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_gain = sd(learning_gain, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n()
  )

cor_group <- cor.test(df_gains_group$Cocreation_Level, df_gains_group$mean_gain,
                     method = "pearson")

cat("\nGROUP-LEVEL CORRELATION:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Pearson r = %.3f\n", cor_group$estimate))
cat(sprintf("  95%% CI: [%.3f, %.3f]\n", cor_group$conf.int[1], cor_group$conf.int[2]))
cat(sprintf("  t(%d) = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", cor_group$parameter, cor_group$statistic, cor_group$p.value))

if (cor_group$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("\n  [SIGNIFICANT] Correlation exists at GROUP level\n")
} else {
  cat("\n  [NOT SIGNIFICANT] No relationship at group level either\n")
}

cat("\n\n=== UNDERSTANDING THE DISCREPANCY ===\n")
cat("Why is co-creation → T7 significant but co-creation → learning gains not?\n\n")

# Calculate correlations for comparison
cor_t7 <- cor.test(df_gains$Cocreation_Level, df_gains$score_t7, method = "pearson")
cor_t1 <- cor.test(df_gains$Cocreation_Level, df_gains$score_t1, method = "pearson")

cat("Simple Correlations:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Co-creation with T1 (baseline): r = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", 
            cor_t1$estimate, cor_t1$p.value))
cat(sprintf("  Co-creation with T7 (post-test): r = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", 
            cor_t7$estimate, cor_t7$p.value))
cat(sprintf("  Co-creation with T1→T7 gain: r = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", 
            cor_result$estimate, cor_result$p.value))

cat("\n")
if (cor_t7$p.value < 0.05 && cor_result$p.value >= 0.05) {
  if (cor_t1$p.value < 0.05) {
    cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
    cat("  -> Co-creation correlates with T7 AND T1 (baseline)\n")
    cat("  -> The T7 effect may partially reflect baseline differences\n")
    cat("  -> Gain scores control for baseline, revealing weaker effect\n")
    cat("  -> However, Model 2 shows T1 doesn't PREDICT co-creation at group level\n")
    cat("  -> This suggests complex clustering patterns\n")
  } else {
    cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
    cat("  -> Co-creation correlates with T7 but NOT with T1 or gains\n")
    cat("  -> This is unusual and warrants investigation\n")
    cat("  -> Possible explanations:\n")
    cat("     1. Non-linear relationship with gains\n")
    cat("     2. Ceiling/floor effects in gain scores\n")
    cat("     3. High within-group variability masking effects\n")
  }
}

cat("\n")
```

### Model 13: Examining Within vs Between Group Effects

```{r model13-variance-decomposition}
cat("\n=== VARIANCE DECOMPOSITION ANALYSIS ===\n")
cat("Why is group-level variance higher for gains than for T7?\n\n")

# Compare ICCs across models
icc_t1 <- as.numeric(performance::icc(lmer(score ~ 1 + (1|Group_factor), 
                                           data = df_long %>% filter(Timepoint == "T1")))$ICC_adjusted)
icc_t7_model1 <- as.numeric(performance::icc(model1)$ICC_adjusted)
icc_gain_model13 <- as.numeric(performance::icc(model13)$ICC_adjusted)

cat("Intraclass Correlations (ICC):\n")
cat(sprintf("  T1 scores (baseline): ICC = %.3f (%.1f%% between groups)\n", 
            icc_t1, icc_t1*100))
cat(sprintf("  T7 scores (Model 1): ICC = %.3f (%.1f%% between groups)\n", 
            icc_t7_model1, icc_t7_model1*100))
cat(sprintf("  Learning gains (Model 13): ICC = %.3f (%.1f%% between groups)\n", 
            icc_gain_model13, icc_gain_model13*100))

cat("\nINTERPRETATION:\n")
if (icc_gain_model13 > icc_t7_model1) {
  cat("  -> Learning GAINS show MORE clustering than absolute T7 scores\n")
  cat("  -> Groups differ more in HOW MUCH students learn than in final scores\n")
  cat("\nPossible explanations:\n")
  cat("  1. GROUP-LEVEL FACTORS affect learning trajectory\n")
  cat("     - Group dynamics, teaching quality, engagement patterns\n")
  cat("     - These create differential learning within groups\n")
  cat("  2. BASELINE HETEROGENEITY within groups\n")
  cat("     - If groups have mixed baseline knowledge, gains vary more\n")
  cat("     - Some students have more 'room to grow'\n")
  cat("  3. MEASUREMENT PROPERTIES\n")
  cat("     - Gain scores may be more sensitive to group processes\n")
  cat("     - Absolute scores may mask within-group learning variation\n")
}

cat("\n\nExamining within-group variance in baseline (T1):\n")

# Calculate within-group SD at T1 and T7
variance_by_group <- df_long %>%
  filter(Timepoint %in% c("T1", "T7")) %>%
  group_by(Group_factor, Timepoint) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_score = mean(score, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_score = sd(score, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Timepoint, 
              values_from = c(mean_score, sd_score, n),
              names_glue = "{.value}_{Timepoint}")

# Merge with co-creation and gains
variance_with_gains <- variance_by_group %>%
  mutate(Group_factor = as.character(Group_factor)) %>%
  left_join(
    df_gains %>% 
      group_by(Group_factor) %>%
      summarise(
        Cocreation_Level = first(Cocreation_Level),
        mean_gain = mean(learning_gain, na.rm = TRUE),
        sd_gain = sd(learning_gain, na.rm = TRUE)
      ) %>%
      mutate(Group_factor = as.character(Group_factor)),
    by = "Group_factor"
  )

cat("\nWithin-group variability by co-creation level:\n")
variance_with_gains %>%
  group_by(Cocreation_Level) %>%
  summarise(
    n_groups = n(),
    mean_SD_T1 = mean(sd_score_T1, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_SD_T7 = mean(sd_score_T7, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_SD_gain = mean(sd_gain, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Mean Within-Group SD by Co-creation Level")

cat("\n")
```

```{r model13-group-patterns, fig.width=14, fig.height=6}
# Visualize group-level patterns
library(gridExtra)

# Plot 1: Group means for learning gains by co-creation
p1 <- ggplot(df_gains_group, aes(x = Cocreation_Level, y = mean_gain)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = n), alpha = 0.7, color = "#f59e0b") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#d97706", fill = "#fbbf24", alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Group_factor), nudge_y = 0.1, size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Group-Level: Mean Learning Gains",
    subtitle = paste0("Group-level correlation: r = ", round(cor_group$estimate, 3),
                     ", p = ", format.pval(cor_group$p.value, digits = 3)),
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Mean Learning Gain per Group",
    size = "Group Size"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

# Plot 2: Within-group variance in gains
p2 <- ggplot(variance_with_gains, aes(x = Cocreation_Level, y = sd_gain)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = n_T1), alpha = 0.7, color = "#8b5cf6") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#6d28d9", fill = "#c4b5fd", alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Group_factor), nudge_y = 0.05, size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Within-Group Variability in Learning Gains",
    subtitle = "Does variability differ by co-creation level?",
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "SD of Learning Gains within Group",
    size = "Group Size"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2,
             top = "Group-Level Analysis: Learning Gains Patterns")
```

```{r model13-individual-vs-group}
# Compare student-level vs group-level relationships
cat("\n=== COMPARING LEVELS OF ANALYSIS ===\n\n")

# Simple regression at student level (ignoring clustering)
lm_student <- lm(learning_gain ~ Cocreation_Level, data = df_gains)

# Simple regression at group level
lm_group <- lm(mean_gain ~ Cocreation_Level, data = df_gains_group)

cat("STUDENT-LEVEL (ignoring groups):\n")
cat(sprintf("  Beta = %.3f, SE = %.3f, p = %.4f\n",
            coef(lm_student)[2],
            summary(lm_student)$coefficients[2, 2],
            summary(lm_student)$coefficients[2, 4]))
cat(sprintf("  R² = %.3f\n", summary(lm_student)$r.squared))

cat("\nGROUP-LEVEL (aggregated):\n")
cat(sprintf("  Beta = %.3f, SE = %.3f, p = %.4f\n",
            coef(lm_group)[2],
            summary(lm_group)$coefficients[2, 2],
            summary(lm_group)$coefficients[2, 4]))
cat(sprintf("  R² = %.3f\n", summary(lm_group)$r.squared))

cat("\nMIXED MODEL (Model 13):\n")
cat(sprintf("  Beta = %.3f, SE = %.3f, p = %.4f\n",
            fixef(model13)[2],
            sqrt(diag(vcov(model13)))[2],
            summary(model13)$coefficients[2, 5]))

cat("\n\nWHY THE DISCREPANCY?\n")
cat("=" %>% strrep(60), "\n")

if (cor_result$p.value >= 0.05 && cor_group$p.value >= 0.05 && p_value_learning_gain >= 0.05) {
  cat("\nAll three approaches show non-significant effects:\n")
  cat("  -> Co-creation does NOT predict learning gains\n")
  cat("  -> The T7 effect (Model 1) must have another explanation\n\n")
  
  cat("POSSIBLE EXPLANATIONS:\n")
  cat("1. CEILING EFFECTS:\n")
  cat("   - If T1 scores are already high, limited room for improvement\n")
  cat("   - Check T1 distribution\n\n")
  
  cat("2. HIGH WITHIN-GROUP VARIABILITY:\n")
  cat("   - ICC = 15.8% means substantial group clustering\n")
  cat("   - But this clustering is not explained by co-creation\n")
  cat("   - Other group factors (teacher, classroom dynamics) matter more\n\n")
  
  cat("3. DIFFERENTIAL BASELINE:\n")
  cat("   - Even without selection effect (Model 2), there may be\n")
  cat("   - random baseline differences between groups\n")
  cat("   - These could affect T7 scores without affecting gains\n\n")
  
  # Check T1 distribution
  cat("Baseline (T1) distribution check:\n")
  t1_summary <- df_gains %>%
    summarise(
      mean_t1 = mean(score_t1),
      ceiling_pct = sum(score_t1 == 2) / n() * 100,
      floor_pct = sum(score_t1 == 0) / n() * 100
    )
  cat(sprintf("  Mean T1 score: %.2f (out of 2)\n", t1_summary$mean_t1))
  cat(sprintf("  At ceiling (score = 2): %.1f%%\n", t1_summary$ceiling_pct))
  cat(sprintf("  At floor (score = 0): %.1f%%\n", t1_summary$floor_pct))
  
  if (t1_summary$mean_t1 > 1.5) {
    cat("\n  -> HIGH baseline scores: ceiling effects may limit gains\n")
  } else if (t1_summary$ceiling_pct > 30) {
    cat("\n  -> Substantial ceiling effects: many students maxed out at T1\n")
  }
}

cat("\n")
```

### Model 13: Exploring the Clustering Pattern

```{r model13-clustering-exploration, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
cat("\n=== WHY IS GROUP-LEVEL VARIANCE HIGH FOR GAINS? ===\n\n")

# Compare variance components across models
cat("Variance Component Comparison:\n")
cat("=" %>% strrep(60), "\n\n")

# Get variance components
get_variance_components <- function(model, model_name) {
  vc <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(model))
  var_group <- vc$vcov[1]
  var_resid <- attr(VarCorr(model), "sc")^2
  icc <- var_group / (var_group + var_resid)
  
  tibble(
    Model = model_name,
    Var_Group = var_group,
    Var_Residual = var_resid,
    ICC = icc,
    Pct_Between = icc * 100
  )
}

variance_comparison <- bind_rows(
  get_variance_components(model1, "Model 1: T7 absolute"),
  get_variance_components(model7, "Model 7: T8 absolute"),
  get_variance_components(model13, "Model 13: T1→T7 gains")
)

variance_comparison %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Variance Components: Why Gains Show More Clustering")

cat("\n\nKEY FINDING:\n")
cat("Learning GAINS show substantially more group-level clustering (ICC = ", 
    round(variance_comparison$ICC[3], 3), ")\n")
cat("than absolute scores at T7 (ICC = ", round(variance_comparison$ICC[1], 3), ")\n\n")

cat("WHAT THIS MEANS:\n")
cat("  1. Groups differ more in LEARNING TRAJECTORIES than in final outcomes\n")
cat("  2. Some groups have consistently higher/lower gains across all students\n")
cat("  3. This group-level variation is NOT explained by co-creation level\n")
cat("  4. Other group factors (not measured) drive learning variation\n\n")

cat("IMPLICATIONS:\n")
cat("  -> Co-creation may not be the primary driver of learning differences\n")
cat("  -> Group context (teacher, dynamics, implementation) matters\n")
cat("  -> The T7 effect (Model 1) may be driven by these unmeasured factors\n")
cat("  -> Caution needed in interpreting co-creation as causal mechanism\n")

# Visualize this
# Create plots showing the clustering
p1 <- ggplot(df_gains, aes(x = factor(Group_factor), y = learning_gain)) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = factor(Cocreation_Level)), alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "Co-creation\nLevel") +
  labs(
    title = "Learning Gains by Group",
    subtitle = "Note the variability between groups",
    x = "Group",
    y = "Learning Gain (T7 - T1)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

p2 <- ggplot(df_gains_group, aes(x = Cocreation_Level, y = sd_gain)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.7, color = "#8b5cf6") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#6d28d9") +
  labs(
    title = "Within-Group Variability vs Co-creation",
    subtitle = "Is variability related to co-creation level?",
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "SD of Learning Gains within Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2,
             top = "Understanding Group-Level Clustering in Learning Gains")
```

```{r model13-interaction-exploration}
cat("\n=== EXPLORING POTENTIAL NON-LINEAR RELATIONSHIPS ===\n\n")

# Check if relationship might be non-linear
model13_quad <- lmer(
  learning_gain ~ Cocreation_Level + I(Cocreation_Level^2) + (1 | Group_factor),
  data = df_gains,
  REML = TRUE
)

cat("Testing for quadratic (non-linear) relationship:\n")
summary(model13_quad)$coefficients %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("Term") %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Quadratic Model: Learning Gain ~ Co-creation + Co-creation²")

# Test if quadratic improves fit
anova_quad <- anova(model13, model13_quad)
p_quad <- anova_quad$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2]

cat(sprintf("\nLikelihood ratio test: p = %.4f\n", p_quad))
if (p_quad < 0.05) {
  cat("  -> QUADRATIC term is significant\n")
  cat("  -> Relationship between co-creation and gains is NON-LINEAR\n")
} else {
  cat("  -> NO evidence of non-linear relationship\n")
}

cat("\n")
```

### Model 13: Data Structure and Power Analysis

```{r model13-data-structure}
cat("\n=== DATA STRUCTURE ANALYSIS ===\n")
cat("Understanding sample distribution and statistical power\n\n")

# Distribution of co-creation levels
cat("Distribution of Co-creation Levels:\n")
df_gains %>%
  group_by(Cocreation_Level) %>%
  summarise(
    n_students = n(),
    n_groups = n_distinct(Group_factor),
    mean_gain = mean(learning_gain),
    sd_gain = sd(learning_gain)
  ) %>%
  kable(digits = 2, caption = "Sample Size by Co-creation Level")

# Check if uneven distribution might affect power
cat("\n")
if (min(table(df_gains$Cocreation_Level)) < 5) {
  cat("[WARNING] Small sample sizes at some co-creation levels\n")
  cat("  -> May limit statistical power to detect effects\n")
}

# Examine relationship accounting for baseline (ANCOVA approach)
cat("\n\n=== ALTERNATIVE APPROACH: ANCOVA ===\n")
cat("Does controlling for T1 as covariate change interpretation?\n\n")

model13_ancova <- lmer(
  score_t7 ~ score_t1 + Cocreation_Level + (1 | Group_factor),
  data = df_gains,
  REML = TRUE
)

cat("T7 Score ~ T1 Score + Co-creation Level:\n")
tidy(model13_ancova, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "ANCOVA: T7 controlling for T1")

beta_cc_ancova <- fixef(model13_ancova)["Cocreation_Level"]
p_cc_ancova <- summary(model13_ancova)$coefficients["Cocreation_Level", "Pr(>|t|)"]

cat("\nComparing approaches:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Simple T7 model (Model 1): beta = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", 
            fixef(model1)[2], summary(model1)$coefficients[2, 5]))
cat(sprintf("  Learning gain model (Model 13): beta = %.3f, p = %.4f\n",
            fixef(model13)[2], summary(model13)$coefficients[2, 5]))
cat(sprintf("  ANCOVA (T7 ~ T1 + CC): beta = %.3f, p = %.4f\n",
            beta_cc_ancova, p_cc_ancova))

cat("\nINTERPRETATION:\n")
if (p_cc_ancova < 0.05 && p_value_learning_gain >= 0.05) {
  cat("  -> ANCOVA shows significant effect when controlling for T1\n")
  cat("  -> But gain scores (which also control for T1) show no effect\n")
  cat("  -> This discrepancy suggests:\n")
  cat("     1. The relationship may not be strictly linear\n")
  cat("     2. Regression to the mean may be occurring\n")
  cat("     3. Gain scores and ANCOVA capture different aspects\n")
} else if (abs(beta_cc_ancova - fixef(model13)[2]) < 0.05) {
  cat("  -> ANCOVA and gain model give similar results\n")
  cat("  -> Consistent evidence for (non-)effect of co-creation on learning\n")
}

cat("\n")
```

```{r model13-regression-to-mean}
cat("\n=== CHECKING FOR REGRESSION TO THE MEAN ===\n\n")

# Regression to the mean: students with low T1 should show higher gains
cor_t1_gain <- cor.test(df_gains$score_t1, df_gains$learning_gain)

cat("Correlation between T1 (baseline) and Learning Gain:\n")
cat(sprintf("  r = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", cor_t1_gain$estimate, cor_t1_gain$p.value))

if (cor_t1_gain$estimate < -0.2 && cor_t1_gain$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("\n  [DETECTED] Regression to the mean is occurring\n")
  cat("  -> Students with low baseline show higher gains\n")
  cat("  -> Students with high baseline show lower gains (ceiling effect)\n")
  cat("  -> This is expected and complicates gain score interpretation\n")
} else {
  cat("\n  [NOT DETECTED] No strong regression to the mean\n")
}

# Visualize
ggplot(df_gains, aes(x = score_t1, y = learning_gain, color = factor(Cocreation_Level))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_color_viridis_d(name = "Co-creation\nLevel") +
  labs(
    title = "Learning Gains vs Baseline Knowledge",
    subtitle = "Testing for regression to the mean by co-creation level",
    x = "T1 Score (Baseline)",
    y = "Learning Gain (T7 - T1)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

```{r model13-sample-size-comparison}
cat("\n=== CRITICAL: SAMPLE SIZE COMPARISON ===\n")
cat("Checking if reduced sample size explains non-significance\n\n")

# Get sample sizes for each model
n_t1 <- df_long %>% filter(Timepoint == "T1") %>% nrow()
n_t7 <- df_long %>% filter(Timepoint == "T7") %>% nrow()
n_gains <- nrow(df_gains)

cat("Sample Sizes Across Models:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Model 2 (T1 only): n = %d students\n", n_t1))
cat(sprintf("  Model 1 (T7 only): n = %d students\n", n_t7))
cat(sprintf("  Model 13 (T1→T7 gains): n = %d students\n", n_gains))

cat("\n[IMPORTANT] Model 1 and Model 13 have IDENTICAL sample sizes!\n")
cat("  -> Sample size reduction is NOT the explanation\n")
cat("  -> Both models use the same 24 students with complete T7 data\n")

# Check which students are in which models
students_t1 <- df_long %>% filter(Timepoint == "T1") %>% pull(`Participant ID`)
students_t7 <- df_long %>% filter(Timepoint == "T7") %>% pull(`Participant ID`)
students_gains <- df_gains %>% pull(`Participant ID`)

# Students at T1 but not at T7
missing_at_t7 <- setdiff(students_t1, students_t7)
# Students at T7 but not at T1  
missing_at_t1 <- setdiff(students_t7, students_t1)

if (length(missing_at_t7) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("\n%d students present at T1 but ABSENT at T7:\n", length(missing_at_t7)))
  df_missing_t7 <- df_long %>%
    filter(`Participant ID` %in% missing_at_t7, Timepoint == "T1") %>%
    select(`Participant ID`, Group_factor, Cocreation_Level, score) %>%
    arrange(Cocreation_Level)
  print(kable(df_missing_t7, caption = "Students who left after T1"))
}

if (length(missing_at_t1) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("\n%d students present at T7 but ABSENT at T1:\n", length(missing_at_t1)))
  df_missing_t1 <- df_long %>%
    filter(`Participant ID` %in% missing_at_t1, Timepoint == "T7") %>%
    select(`Participant ID`, Group_factor, Cocreation_Level, score) %>%
    arrange(Cocreation_Level)
  print(kable(df_missing_t1, caption = "Students who joined after T1 (not in gains)"))
}

cat("\nSample composition:\n")
cat("  Model 13 includes: ALL students present at both T1 AND T7\n")
cat("  Model 1 includes: ALL students present at T7 (regardless of T1)\n")

if (n_gains == n_t7) {
  cat("\n[CRITICAL] Sample sizes are IDENTICAL\n")
  cat("  -> Power differences must come from OTHER sources\n")
}

cat("\n")
```

```{r model13-attendance-analysis}
cat("\n=== STUDENT ATTENDANCE PATTERN ANALYSIS ===\n")
cat("Why do we have 27 students with co-creation but only 24 at T7?\n\n")

# Get all unique students across all timepoints
all_students <- df_long %>%
  pull(`Participant ID`) %>%
  unique()

# Create attendance matrix
attendance <- df_long %>%
  select(`Participant ID`, Timepoint, Group_factor, Cocreation_Level) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  mutate(present = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Timepoint,
              values_from = present,
              values_fill = 0) %>%
  arrange(`Participant ID`)

cat("Complete Attendance Pattern:\n")
cat("=" %>% strrep(60), "\n")
cat(sprintf("Total unique students in dataset: %d\n", nrow(attendance)))
cat(sprintf("Students at T1: %d\n", sum(attendance$T1)))
cat(sprintf("Students at T4: %d\n", sum(attendance$T4)))
cat(sprintf("Students at T7: %d\n", sum(attendance$T7)))
cat(sprintf("Students at T8: %d\n", sum(attendance$T8)))

# Identify students missing T7
students_missing_t7 <- attendance %>%
  filter(T7 == 0)

if (nrow(students_missing_t7) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("\n[FOUND] %d students have co-creation data but NO T7 scores:\n\n", 
              nrow(students_missing_t7)))
  
  students_missing_t7 %>%
    select(`Participant ID`, Group_factor, Cocreation_Level, T1, T4, T7, T8) %>%
    kable(caption = "Students Missing T7 Data (1 = present, 0 = absent)")
  
  cat("\n")
  cat("These students are EXCLUDED from:\n")
  cat("  - Model 1 (T7 analysis)\n")
  cat("  - Model 13 (learning gains T1→T7)\n")
  cat("  - Any other T7-based analysis\n\n")
  
  # Check their co-creation distribution
  cat("Co-creation distribution of missing students:\n")
  students_missing_t7 %>%
    group_by(Cocreation_Level) %>%
    summarise(n = n()) %>%
    kable()
  
  cat("\n")
  
  # Check if they were present at other timepoints
  cat("Their attendance at other timepoints:\n")
  cat(sprintf("  Present at T1: %d/%d\n", sum(students_missing_t7$T1), nrow(students_missing_t7)))
  cat(sprintf("  Present at T4: %d/%d\n", sum(students_missing_t7$T4), nrow(students_missing_t7)))
  cat(sprintf("  Present at T8: %d/%d\n", sum(students_missing_t7$T8), nrow(students_missing_t7)))
}

# Show complete attendance patterns
cat("\n\nFull Attendance Pattern Summary:\n")
cat("=" %>% strrep(60), "\n")

attendance_patterns <- attendance %>%
  mutate(
    pattern = paste(T1, T4, T7, T8, sep = "-"),
    complete = (T1 == 1 & T4 == 1 & T7 == 1 & T8 == 1)
  ) %>%
  group_by(pattern) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    description = case_when(
      pattern == "1-1-1-1" ~ "All timepoints",
      pattern == "1-1-1-0" ~ "Missing T8 only",
      pattern == "1-1-0-1" ~ "Missing T7 only",
      pattern == "1-1-0-0" ~ "Missing T7 & T8",
      pattern == "1-0-1-1" ~ "Missing T4 only",
      pattern == "0-1-1-1" ~ "Missing T1 only",
      TRUE ~ "Other pattern"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n))

print(kable(attendance_patterns, caption = "Student Attendance Patterns (1=present, 0=absent)"))

cat("\n")
cat("IMPLICATIONS FOR ANALYSIS:\n")
cat(sprintf("  - Complete longitudinal data: %d students\n", 
            sum(attendance$T1 & attendance$T4 & attendance$T7 & attendance$T8)))
cat(sprintf("  - Can calculate T1→T7 gains: %d students\n", n_gains))
cat(sprintf("  - Can calculate T7→T8 change: %d students\n", 
            nrow(df_change)))

cat("\n")
```

```{r model13-verification-t1-in-t7}
cat("\n=== CRITICAL VERIFICATION: Do all T7 students have T1 data? ===\n")
cat("This explains why Model 1 and Model 13 have identical sample sizes\n\n")

# Get students at each timepoint
students_t1 <- df_long %>% filter(Timepoint == "T1") %>% pull(`Participant ID`)
students_t7 <- df_long %>% filter(Timepoint == "T7") %>% pull(`Participant ID`)

cat(sprintf("Students at T1: %d\n", length(students_t1)))
cat(sprintf("Students at T7: %d\n", length(students_t7)))

# Check: do all T7 students also have T1?
t7_students_with_t1 <- students_t7[students_t7 %in% students_t1]
t7_students_without_t1 <- setdiff(students_t7, students_t1)

cat(sprintf("\nT7 students who ALSO have T1: %d\n", length(t7_students_with_t1)))
cat(sprintf("T7 students who LACK T1: %d\n", length(t7_students_without_t1)))

if (length(t7_students_without_t1) == 0) {
  cat("\n[CONFIRMED] ALL T7 students also have T1 data!\n")
  cat("  -> This explains why Model 1 (n=24) and Model 13 (n=24) are identical\n")
  cat("  -> Every student at T7 can be included in gain analysis\n")
  cat("  -> Sample size is NOT the issue for Model 13 non-significance\n")
} else {
  cat("\n[UNEXPECTED] Some T7 students don't have T1 data\n")
  cat("  -> These students should be excluded from Model 13\n")
  
  df_t7_no_t1 <- df_long %>%
    filter(`Participant ID` %in% t7_students_without_t1, Timepoint == "T7") %>%
    select(`Participant ID`, Group_factor, Cocreation_Level, score)
  
  print(kable(df_t7_no_t1, caption = "T7 students without T1 data"))
}

# Now identify the 3 students with co-creation but no T7
all_students <- df_long %>% distinct(`Participant ID`) %>% pull(`Participant ID`)
students_no_t7 <- setdiff(all_students, students_t7)

cat(sprintf("\n\n=== STUDENTS WITH CO-CREATION DATA BUT NO T7 ===\n"))
cat(sprintf("Total students in dataset: %d\n", length(all_students)))
cat(sprintf("Students missing T7: %d\n\n", length(students_no_t7)))

if (length(students_no_t7) > 0) {
  # Get their info from any available timepoint
  df_no_t7 <- df_long %>%
    filter(`Participant ID` %in% students_no_t7) %>%
    group_by(`Participant ID`) %>%
    summarise(
      Group_factor = first(Group_factor),
      Cocreation_Level = first(Cocreation_Level),
      has_T1 = any(Timepoint == "T1"),
      has_T4 = any(Timepoint == "T4"),
      has_T7 = any(Timepoint == "T7"),
      has_T8 = any(Timepoint == "T8"),
      .groups = 'drop'
    ) %>%
    arrange(Cocreation_Level, Group_factor)
  
  print(kable(df_no_t7, caption = "Students Who Never Showed Up for T7 Testing"))
  
  cat("\nThese students are EXCLUDED from ALL T7-based analyses:\n")
  cat("  - Model 1 (T7 absolute scores)\n")
  cat("  - Model 8 (T7 LLM questions)\n")
  cat("  - Model 10 (T7 combined knowledge)\n")
  cat("  - Model 13 (T1→T7 learning gains)\n")
  
  # Check their co-creation distribution
  cat("\n\nCo-creation distribution of students missing T7:\n")
  df_no_t7 %>%
    group_by(Cocreation_Level) %>%
    summarise(n = n()) %>%
    kable()
  
  # Check if this creates bias
  cat("\n")
  if (max(df_no_t7$Cocreation_Level) - min(df_no_t7$Cocreation_Level) > 1) {
    cat("[WARNING] Missing students span multiple co-creation levels\n")
    cat("  -> Could introduce bias if missingness related to co-creation\n")
  } else {
    cat("[OK] Missing students concentrated in one co-creation level\n")
    cat("  -> Less likely to bias results\n")
  }
}

cat("\n\nSUMMARY:\n")
cat("=" %>% strrep(60), "\n")
cat(sprintf("Starting sample: %d students with co-creation assignments\n", length(all_students)))
cat(sprintf("Attended T7: %d students (%.1f%%)\n", length(students_t7), 
            (length(students_t7)/length(all_students))*100))
cat(sprintf("Missing T7: %d students (%.1f%%)\n", length(students_no_t7),
            (length(students_no_t7)/length(all_students))*100))

if (length(t7_students_without_t1) == 0) {
  cat(sprintf("\nAll %d T7 students have T1 data → Model 1 and Model 13 use same sample\n", 
              length(students_t7)))
}

cat("\n")
```

```{r model13-power-analysis}

cat("\n=== STATISTICAL POWER CONSIDERATIONS ===\n\n")

# Calculate effect size (Cohen's f²)
r2_marginal <- r2(model13)$R2_marginal
f_squared <- r2_marginal / (1 - r2_marginal)

cat("Effect Size:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Marginal R² = %.3f\n", r2_marginal))
cat(sprintf("  Cohen's f² = %.3f\n", f_squared))

if (f_squared < 0.02) {
  cat("  -> VERY SMALL effect size\n")
} else if (f_squared < 0.15) {
  cat("  -> SMALL effect size\n")
} else if (f_squared < 0.35) {
  cat("  -> MEDIUM effect size\n")
} else {
  cat("  -> LARGE effect size\n")
}

cat("\nSample Size:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Model 1 (T7): n = %d students\n", n_t7))
cat(sprintf("  Model 13 (gains): n = %d students\n", nrow(df_gains)))

if (n_gains == n_t7) {
  cat("\n  [IDENTICAL SAMPLE SIZES] Both models use the same 24 students\n")
  cat("  -> Sample size is NOT the reason for different p-values\n\n")
} else {
  cat(sprintf("  Sample size difference: %d students\n", abs(n_t7 - n_gains)))
}

cat(sprintf("  Number of groups: %d\n", n_distinct(df_gains$Group_factor)))
cat(sprintf("  Students per group: %.1f (mean)\n", 
            nrow(df_gains) / n_distinct(df_gains$Group_factor)))

# Compare standard errors - THIS is the key
se_model1 <- sqrt(diag(vcov(model1)))[2]
se_model13 <- sqrt(diag(vcov(model13)))[2]

cat("\nSTANDARD ERROR COMPARISON:\n")
cat(sprintf("  SE for Model 1 (T7): %.3f\n", se_model1))
cat(sprintf("  SE for Model 13 (gains): %.3f\n", se_model13))
cat(sprintf("  SE ratio: %.2fx larger for gains model\n\n", se_model13 / se_model1))

cat("WHY IS THE STANDARD ERROR %.2fx LARGER?\n", se_model13 / se_model1)
cat("=" %>% strrep(60), "\n\n")

cat("1. HIGHER VARIABILITY IN OUTCOME:\n")
cat("   - Strong regression to mean (r = -0.732)\n")
cat("   - Gain scores inherently more variable than levels\n")
cat(sprintf("   - SD of T7 scores: %.3f\n", sd(df_gains$score_t7, na.rm = TRUE)))
cat(sprintf("   - SD of learning gains: %.3f\n", sd(df_gains$learning_gain, na.rm = TRUE)))
cat(sprintf("   - Gain SD is %.2fx larger\n\n", 
            sd(df_gains$learning_gain, na.rm = TRUE) / sd(df_gains$score_t7, na.rm = TRUE)))

cat("2. HIGHER ICC (LESS EFFICIENT SAMPLE):\n")
icc_model1 <- as.numeric(performance::icc(model1)$ICC_adjusted)
icc_model13 <- as.numeric(performance::icc(model13)$ICC_adjusted)
cat(sprintf("   - Model 1 ICC: %.1f%%\n", icc_model1 * 100))
cat(sprintf("   - Model 13 ICC: %.1f%%\n", icc_model13 * 100))
cat("   - Higher ICC = more clustering = less independent information\n")
cat(sprintf("   - Effective sample size reduced by clustering\n\n"))

# Calculate "effective" sample size accounting for ICC
# Design effect = 1 + (avg cluster size - 1) * ICC
avg_cluster_size <- nrow(df_gains) / n_distinct(df_gains$Group_factor)
design_effect_model1 <- 1 + (avg_cluster_size - 1) * icc_model1
design_effect_model13 <- 1 + (avg_cluster_size - 1) * icc_model13

eff_n_model1 <- n_t7 / design_effect_model1
eff_n_model13 <- n_gains / design_effect_model13

cat("3. EFFECTIVE SAMPLE SIZE (accounting for clustering):\n")
cat(sprintf("   - Model 1 effective n: %.1f (design effect = %.2f)\n", 
            eff_n_model1, design_effect_model1))
cat(sprintf("   - Model 13 effective n: %.1f (design effect = %.2f)\n",
            eff_n_model13, design_effect_model13))
cat(sprintf("   - Model 13 has %.1f%% less effective sample\n\n",
            (1 - eff_n_model13/eff_n_model1) * 100))

# Rule of thumb for mixed models: need ~30 groups for stable estimates
if (n_distinct(df_gains$Group_factor) < 30) {
  cat("  [NOTE] With <30 groups, mixed model estimates may be unstable\n")
  cat("  -> Group-level effects should be interpreted cautiously\n")
}

cat("\n\nBOTTOM LINE:\n")
cat("=" %>% strrep(60), "\n")
cat("The non-significant gain effect is due to STATISTICAL PROPERTIES OF GAIN SCORES:\n\n")

cat("1. SAME SAMPLE SIZE (n = 24 for both models):\n")
cat("   - Sample size reduction is NOT the explanation\n\n")

cat("2. MUCH HIGHER VARIABILITY:\n")
cat("   - Strong regression to mean (r = -0.732)\n")
cat("   - Gain scores are %.2fx more variable than T7 scores\n", 
    sd(df_gains$learning_gain, na.rm = TRUE) / sd(df_gains$score_t7, na.rm = TRUE))
cat(sprintf("   - SE is %.2fx larger than Model 1\n\n", se_model13 / se_model1))

cat("3. HIGHER ICC (15.8% vs 2.9%):\n")
cat("   - More clustering = less 'effective' sample size\n")
cat(sprintf("   - Effective n reduced by %.1f%% due to clustering\n", 
            (1 - eff_n_model13/eff_n_model1) * 100))
cat("   - Groups differ in learning trajectories (not explained by co-creation)\n")
cat("   - Other group factors create 'noise' in the gain model\n\n")

cat("4. SIMILAR EFFECT SIZE:\n")
cat("   - Beta coefficients are nearly identical (0.362 vs 0.339)\n")
cat("   - ANCOVA (T7 ~ T1 + CC) is highly significant (p = 0.0061)\n")
cat("   - This suggests the EFFECT EXISTS but gain model lacks power\n\n")

cat("CONCLUSION:\n")
cat("Co-creation likely DOES affect learning gains, but Model 13 is\n")
cat("underpowered to detect it due to:\n")
cat("  - High variability from regression to mean\n")
cat("  - Substantial unexplained group-level variance (ICC = 15.8%)\n")
cat("  - Reduced effective sample size from clustering\n\n")
cat("The ANCOVA approach (p = 0.0061) provides stronger evidence because:\n")
cat("  - Same sample size but lower variability\n")
cat("  - Better handles regression to mean\n")
cat("  - More efficient use of the data structure\n")

cat("\n")
```

### Model 13 Visualizations

```{r model13-plots, fig.width=14, fig.height=6}
library(gridExtra)

# Plot 1: Predicted learning gains by co-creation
p1 <- plot_model(model13, type = "pred", terms = "Cocreation_Level") +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Learning Gains by Co-creation Level",
    subtitle = paste0("Model 13: beta = ", round(beta_learning_gain, 3), 
                     ", p = ", format.pval(p_value_learning_gain, digits = 3)),
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Learning Gain (T7 - T1)"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", alpha = 0.5) +
  ylim(-0.5, 2) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

# Plot 2: Raw data
p2 <- ggplot(df_gains, aes(x = Cocreation_Level, y = learning_gain)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, height = 0.05, alpha = 0.6, size = 3, color = "#f59e0b") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#d97706", fill = "#fbbf24", alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Raw Data: Learning Gains by Co-creation",
    subtitle = "Points above red line = positive learning",
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Learning Gain (T7 - T1)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2,
             top = "Learning Gains Analysis (T1 → T7): Co-creation Effect")
```

```{r model13-trajectories, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
cat("\n=== TRAJECTORY PLOT DIAGNOSTICS ===\n")

# Get total students with co-creation data
total_students_with_cocreation <- df_long %>% 
  distinct(`Participant ID`) %>% 
  nrow()

# Check how many students we should plot
cat(sprintf("Total students with co-creation data: %d\n", total_students_with_cocreation))
cat(sprintf("Students at T1: %d\n", n_t1))
cat(sprintf("Students at T7: %d\n", n_t7))
cat(sprintf("Students in df_gains (have both T1 AND T7): %d\n", nrow(df_gains)))

students_missing_from_plot <- total_students_with_cocreation - nrow(df_gains)

cat(sprintf("\nStudents excluded from trajectory plot: %d (%.1f%% of total)\n", 
            students_missing_from_plot, 
            (students_missing_from_plot / total_students_with_cocreation) * 100))

# Identify who is missing from the plot
students_all <- df_long %>% distinct(`Participant ID`) %>% pull(`Participant ID`)
students_in_plot <- df_gains %>% pull(`Participant ID`)
students_excluded <- setdiff(students_all, students_in_plot)

if (length(students_excluded) > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("\n%d students have co-creation data but not in trajectory plot:\n", 
              length(students_excluded)))
  
  # Find their attendance pattern
  df_excluded_attendance <- df_long %>%
    filter(`Participant ID` %in% students_excluded) %>%
    group_by(`Participant ID`) %>%
    summarise(
      Group_factor = first(Group_factor),
      Cocreation_Level = first(Cocreation_Level),
      has_T1 = any(Timepoint == "T1"),
      has_T4 = any(Timepoint == "T4"),
      has_T7 = any(Timepoint == "T7"),
      has_T8 = any(Timepoint == "T8")
    ) %>%
    arrange(Cocreation_Level, Group_factor)
  
  print(kable(df_excluded_attendance, 
              caption = "Students Excluded from T1→T7 Trajectory Plot"))
  
  cat("\nReason for exclusion:\n")
  if (!all(df_excluded_attendance$has_T7)) {
    cat("  -> Missing T7 data (not present at post-test)\n")
  }
  if (!all(df_excluded_attendance$has_T1)) {
    cat("  -> Missing T1 data (not present at baseline)\n")
  }
}

# Show T1 and T7 scores by co-creation level
df_gains_long <- df_gains %>%
  select(Cocreation_Level, score_t1, score_t7, `Participant ID`, Group_factor) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(score_t1, score_t7),
               names_to = "Timepoint",
               values_to = "Score") %>%
  mutate(
    Timepoint = factor(Timepoint, 
                      levels = c("score_t1", "score_t7"),
                      labels = c("T1 (Baseline)", "T7 (Post-test)"))
  )

cat(sprintf("\nStudents actually plotted: %d\n", n_distinct(df_gains_long$`Participant ID`)))

# Check distribution by co-creation level
cat("\nStudents plotted by co-creation level:\n")
df_gains %>%
  group_by(Cocreation_Level) %>%
  summarise(n = n()) %>%
  kable()

# Check for any missing values that would break lines
students_with_missing <- df_gains %>%
  filter(is.na(score_t1) | is.na(score_t7)) %>%
  nrow()

if (students_with_missing > 0) {
  cat(sprintf("\n[WARNING] %d students have missing T1 or T7 data\n", students_with_missing))
  cat("  -> These students won't show connected lines\n")
} else {
  cat("\n[OK] All students in df_gains have complete T1 and T7 data\n")
  cat("    -> All 24 students will show as connected lines\n")
}

cat("\n")

# Create plot with detailed annotation
p_main <- ggplot(df_gains_long, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Score, group = `Participant ID`)) +
  geom_line(aes(color = factor(Cocreation_Level)), alpha = 0.4, size = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(color = factor(Cocreation_Level)), alpha = 0.6, size = 2.5) +
  stat_summary(aes(group = Cocreation_Level, color = factor(Cocreation_Level)),
               fun = mean, geom = "line", size = 2.5, alpha = 1) +
  stat_summary(aes(group = Cocreation_Level, color = factor(Cocreation_Level)),
               fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 5, shape = 18) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(name = "Co-creation\nLevel") +
  annotate("text", x = 1.5, y = max(df_gains_long$Score, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.1, 
           label = sprintf("n = %d students\n(out of %d with co-creation data)", 
                          n_distinct(df_gains_long$`Participant ID`),
                          total_students_with_cocreation),
           size = 4.5, hjust = 0.5, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(
    title = "Learning Trajectories: T1 → T7 by Co-creation Level",
    subtitle = sprintf("Thin lines = individual students (n=%d); Thick lines = group means", 
                      n_distinct(df_gains_long$`Participant ID`)),
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Knowledge Score (0-2)",
    caption = sprintf("Note: %d/%d students shown (%.1f%% with complete T1 & T7 data)", 
                     nrow(df_gains), total_students_with_cocreation, 
                     (nrow(df_gains)/total_students_with_cocreation)*100)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10, face = "italic"))

print(p_main)

# Additional: Show sample sizes by co-creation in table overlay
cat("\nSample breakdown by co-creation level (for this plot):\n")
df_gains %>%
  group_by(Cocreation_Level) %>%
  summarise(
    n_students = n(),
    mean_T1 = mean(score_t1),
    mean_T7 = mean(score_t7),
    mean_gain = mean(learning_gain)
  ) %>%
  kable(digits = 2, caption = "Learning Trajectory Statistics by Co-creation Level")
```

```{r model13-gain-distribution, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# Distribution of gains by co-creation level
ggplot(df_gains, aes(x = factor(Cocreation_Level), y = learning_gain)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 1) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "#fbbf24", alpha = 0.6, outlier.alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.05, alpha = 0.4, size = 2, color = "#d97706") +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 23, size = 4, 
               fill = "red", color = "darkred") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Learning Gains by Co-creation Level",
    subtitle = "Red diamond = mean; Red line = no learning",
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Learning Gain (T7 - T1)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

### Comparative Visualization: T7 vs T8

```{r model1-model7-comparison, fig.width=14, fig.height=6}
# Create comparison plots for T7 vs T8
library(gridExtra)

# T7 plot
p_t7 <- plot_model(model1, type = "pred", terms = "Cocreation_Level") +
  labs(
    title = "T7: Immediate Post-test",
    subtitle = paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model1)[2], 3), 
                     ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model1))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Predicted Knowledge Score"
  ) +
  ylim(0, 2) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# T8 plot
p_t8 <- plot_model(model7, type = "pred", terms = "Cocreation_Level") +
  labs(
    title = "T8: Delayed Follow-up",
    subtitle = paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model7)[2], 3), 
                     ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model7))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Predicted Knowledge Score"
  ) +
  ylim(0, 2) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# Arrange side by side
grid.arrange(p_t7, p_t8, ncol = 2,
             top = "Comparison: Co-creation Effects on Immediate vs Delayed Learning")
```

```{r t7-t8-raw-data-comparison, fig.width=14, fig.height=6}
# Raw data comparison with regression lines
df_t7_t8 <- df_long %>% filter(Timepoint %in% c("T7", "T8"))

ggplot(df_t7_t8, aes(x = Cocreation_Level, y = score)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Timepoint_factor), width = 0.1, height = 0, alpha = 0.6, size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color = Timepoint_factor, fill = Timepoint_factor), 
              method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("T7" = "#10b981", "T8" = "#8b5cf6"),
                    labels = c("T7 (Immediate)", "T8 (Delayed)")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("T7" = "#10b981", "T8" = "#8b5cf6"),
                   labels = c("T7 (Immediate)", "T8 (Delayed)")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.1, 2.1), breaks = seq(0, 2, 0.5)) +
  labs(
    title = "Co-creation Effects: Immediate (T7) vs Delayed (T8) Post-test",
    subtitle = "Raw data with linear regression lines",
    x = "Co-creation Level",
    y = "Knowledge Score (0-2)",
    color = "Timepoint",
    fill = "Timepoint"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

### Summary: Persistence of Co-creation Benefits

```{r t7-t8-summary-stats}
cat("\n=== T7 vs T8 SUMMARY ===\n\n")

# Extract and compare key statistics
t7_stats <- tibble(
  Timepoint = "T7 (Immediate)",
  Beta = fixef(model1)["Cocreation_Level"],
  SE = sqrt(diag(vcov(model1)))["Cocreation_Level"],
  CI_lower = confint(model1, parm = "Cocreation_Level")[1],
  CI_upper = confint(model1, parm = "Cocreation_Level")[2],
  p_value = summary(model1)$coefficients["Cocreation_Level", "Pr(>|t|)"],
  R2_marginal = r2(model1)$R2_marginal
)

t8_stats <- tibble(
  Timepoint = "T8 (Delayed)",
  Beta = fixef(model7)["Cocreation_Level"],
  SE = sqrt(diag(vcov(model7)))["Cocreation_Level"],
  CI_lower = confint(model7, parm = "Cocreation_Level")[1],
  CI_upper = confint(model7, parm = "Cocreation_Level")[2],
  p_value = summary(model7)$coefficients["Cocreation_Level", "Pr(>|t|)"],
  R2_marginal = r2(model7)$R2_marginal
)

comparison_table <- bind_rows(t7_stats, t8_stats)

comparison_table %>%
  mutate(
    `95% CI` = sprintf("[%.3f, %.3f]", CI_lower, CI_upper),
    Significant = ifelse(p_value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
  ) %>%
  select(Timepoint, Beta, SE, `95% CI`, p_value, Significant, R2_marginal) %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Comparison of Co-creation Effects at T7 vs T8")

# Calculate effect retention
if (t7_stats$p_value < 0.05 && t8_stats$p_value < 0.05) {
  retention_pct <- (t8_stats$Beta / t7_stats$Beta) * 100
  cat(sprintf("\nEffect Retention: T8 effect is %.1f%% of T7 effect\n", retention_pct))
  
  if (retention_pct > 90) {
    cat("Interpretation: EXCELLENT retention - Co-creation benefits fully maintained\n")
  } else if (retention_pct > 70) {
    cat("Interpretation: GOOD retention - Co-creation benefits largely maintained\n")
  } else if (retention_pct > 50) {
    cat("Interpretation: MODERATE retention - Co-creation benefits partially maintained\n")
  } else {
    cat("Interpretation: WEAK retention - Substantial decay in co-creation benefits\n")
  }
}

cat("\n========================\n")
```


# Model Comparison

```{r model-comparison}
# Compare models with same outcome (basic microcontroller scores)
cat("=== BASIC MICROCONTROLLER MODELS (2 questions) ===\n")
comparison_basic <- compare_performance(model1, model3, model4, model5, model7, verbose = FALSE)

comparison_basic %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Model Comparison: Basic Microcontroller Questions (2 items)")

cat("\n\n=== LLM MICROCONTROLLER MODELS (8 questions) ===\n")
comparison_llm <- compare_performance(model8, model9, verbose = FALSE)

comparison_llm %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Model Comparison: LLM Microcontroller Questions (8 items)")

cat("\n\n=== COMBINED MICROCONTROLLER MODELS (10 questions total) ===\n")
comparison_combined <- compare_performance(model10, model11, verbose = FALSE)

comparison_combined %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Model Comparison: Combined Microcontroller Questions (10 items)")

cat("\n\n=== LEARNING GAINS MODEL (T1 → T7) ===\n")
cat("This model examines learning from baseline to immediate post-test\n\n")

# Create a simple comparison for Model 13
model13_comparison <- tibble(
  Name = "Model 13 (Learning Gains)",
  Model = "lmer",
  AIC = AIC(model13),
  BIC = BIC(model13),
  R2_conditional = r2(model13)$R2_conditional,
  R2_marginal = r2(model13)$R2_marginal
)

model13_comparison %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Model 13: Learning Gains (T1 → T7, Basic 2 items)")

cat("\n\n=== KNOWLEDGE RETENTION/DECAY MODELS (T7 → T8) ===\n")
cat("These models examine retention/decay rather than absolute scores\n\n")
comparison_change <- compare_performance(model12a, model12b, model12c, verbose = FALSE)

comparison_change %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "Model Comparison: Knowledge Change Models (Retention/Decay)")

# AIC/BIC comparison
cat("\n\nInterpretation Notes:\n")
cat("  - Lower AIC/BIC indicates better model fit\n")
cat("  - Models 2 and 6 excluded (different DV: they predict Co-creation level)\n")
cat("  - Basic, LLM, Combined, Gain, and Change models use different outcomes\n")
cat("  - Within each category, compare models to understand co-creation effects\n")
cat("  - Model 13 examines whether co-creation predicts learning gains (T1→T7)\n")
cat("  - Model 12 examines whether co-creation predicts knowledge retention/decay (T7→T8)\n")
```

# Summary of Results

```{r summary-table}
# Create summary table
results_summary <- tibble(
  Model = c(
    "Model 1: Co-creation → T7 (basic, 2 items)",
    "Model 2: T1 → Co-creation (selection)",
    "Model 3: Longitudinal (Time only)",
    "Model 4: Longitudinal + co-creation",
    "Model 5: Time × Co-creation interaction",
    "Model 6: T4 → Co-creation (selection)",
    "Model 7: Co-creation → T8 (basic, 2 items)",
    "Model 8: Co-creation → T7 (LLM, 8 items)",
    "Model 9: Co-creation → T8 (LLM, 8 items)",
    "Model 10: Co-creation → T7 (combined, 10 items)",
    "Model 11: Co-creation → T8 (combined, 10 items)",
    "Model 12a: Co-creation → Retention (basic T7→T8)",
    "Model 12b: Co-creation → Retention (LLM T7→T8)",
    "Model 12c: Co-creation → Retention (combined T7→T8)",
    "Model 13: Co-creation → Learning Gain (basic T1→T7)"
  ),
  Outcome = c(
    "Basic MC (0-2)",
    "Co-creation",
    "Basic MC (0-2)",
    "Basic MC (0-2)",
    "Basic MC (0-2)",
    "Co-creation",
    "Basic MC (0-2)",
    "LLM MC (0-8)",
    "LLM MC (0-8)",
    "Combined (0-10)",
    "Combined (0-10)",
    "Change T7→T8 basic",
    "Change T7→T8 LLM",
    "Change T7→T8 comb",
    "Gain T1→T7 basic"
  ),
  Formula = c(
    "Score ~ Co-creation + (1|Group)",
    "Co-creation ~ T1_Score",
    "Score ~ Time + (1|Group) + (1|Student)",
    "Score ~ Time + Co-creation + (1|Group) + (1|Student)",
    "Score ~ Time × Co-creation + (1|Group) + (1|Student)",
    "Co-creation ~ T4_Score",
    "Score ~ Co-creation + (1|Group)",
    "LLM_Score ~ Co-creation + (1|Group)",
    "LLM_Score ~ Co-creation + (1|Group)",
    "Combined ~ Co-creation + (1|Group)",
    "Combined ~ Co-creation + (1|Group)",
    "Change_T7T8 ~ Co-creation + (1|Group)",
    "Change_LLM_T7T8 ~ Co-creation + (1|Group)",
    "Change_Comb_T7T8 ~ Co-creation + (1|Group)",
    "Gain_T1T7 ~ Co-creation + (1|Group)"
  ),
  
  Key_Finding = c(
  # Model 1: mixed model (T7 basic)
  paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model1)[2], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model1))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
  # Model 2: simple lm (T1 -> Co-creation)
  paste0("beta = ", round(coef(summary(model2))[2, 1], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model2))[2, 4], digits = 3)),
  # Model 3: mixed model (time only)
  paste0("Time beta = ", round(fixef(model3)[2], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model3))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
  # Model 4: mixed model (time + co-creation)
  paste0("CC beta = ", round(fixef(model4)[3], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model4))[3, 5], digits = 3)),
  # Model 5: mixed model (interaction)
  paste0("Interaction = ", round(fixef(model5)[4], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model5))[4, 5], digits = 3)),
  # Model 6: simple lm (T4 -> Co-creation)
  paste0("beta = ", round(coef(summary(model6))[2, 1], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model6))[2, 4], digits = 3)),
  # Model 7: mixed model (T8 basic)
  paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model7)[2], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model7))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
  # Model 8: mixed model (T7 LLM)
  paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model8)[2], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model8))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
  # Model 9: mixed model (T8 LLM)
  paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model9)[2], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model9))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
  # Model 10: mixed model (T7 combined)
  paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model10)[2], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model10))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
  # Model 11: mixed model (T8 combined)
  paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model11)[2], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model11))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
  # Model 12a: mixed model (basic change)
  paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model12a)[2], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model12a))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
  # Model 12b: mixed model (LLM change)
  paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model12b)[2], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model12b))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
  # Model 12c: mixed model (combined change)
  paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model12c)[2], 3), 
         ", p = ", format.pval(coef(summary(model12c))[2, 5], digits = 3)),
  # Model 13: mixed model (learning gains T1→T7)
  paste0("beta = ", round(fixef(model13)[2], 3),
```
